title: "A" Camshaft Position Slow Response (Bank 1)
description: Variable valve timing (VarioCam) fault: during a commanded camshaft phase change, the actual position of the "A" camshaft on bank 1 (cylinders 1-3) responds too slowly to match the position the DME requested. Usually oil-flow or actuator related rather than a failed sensor.
causes: Low oil level, degraded oil, or wrong viscosity
Clogged camshaft oil control valve (VarioCam solenoid) or its filter screen
Stretched timing chain or worn chain tensioner/guides
Wiring or connector fault at the solenoid
diagnosis: Check oil level and condition; confirm correct grade (Porsche A40 / 0W-40)
Scan live data: compare commanded vs actual cam timing during a phase change
Test the camshaft oil control valve solenoid (resistance + actuation) and clean its screen
Inspect timing chain for stretch and the tensioner/guides for wear
Check solenoid wiring/connector continuity and the cam sensor signal
Clear the code and road-test to confirm the fix

title: "B" Camshaft Position Slow Response (Bank 1)
description: Variable valve timing (VarioCam) fault: during a commanded camshaft phase change, the actual position of the "B" camshaft on bank 1 (cylinders 1-3) responds too slowly to match the position the DME requested. Usually oil-flow or actuator related rather than a failed sensor.
causes: Low oil level, degraded oil, or wrong viscosity
Clogged camshaft oil control valve (VarioCam solenoid) or its filter screen
Stretched timing chain or worn chain tensioner/guides
Wiring or connector fault at the solenoid
diagnosis: Check oil level and condition; confirm correct grade (Porsche A40 / 0W-40)
Scan live data: compare commanded vs actual cam timing during a phase change
Test the camshaft oil control valve solenoid (resistance + actuation) and clean its screen
Inspect timing chain for stretch and the tensioner/guides for wear
Check solenoid wiring/connector continuity and the cam sensor signal
Clear the code and road-test to confirm the fix

title: "A" Camshaft Position Slow Response (Bank 2)
description: Variable valve timing (VarioCam) fault: during a commanded camshaft phase change, the actual position of the "A" camshaft on bank 2 (cylinders 4-6) responds too slowly to match the position the DME requested. Usually oil-flow or actuator related rather than a failed sensor.
causes: Low oil level, degraded oil, or wrong viscosity
Clogged camshaft oil control valve (VarioCam solenoid) or its filter screen
Stretched timing chain or worn chain tensioner/guides
Wiring or connector fault at the solenoid
diagnosis: Check oil level and condition; confirm correct grade (Porsche A40 / 0W-40)
Scan live data: compare commanded vs actual cam timing during a phase change
Test the camshaft oil control valve solenoid (resistance + actuation) and clean its screen
Inspect timing chain for stretch and the tensioner/guides for wear
Check solenoid wiring/connector continuity and the cam sensor signal
Clear the code and road-test to confirm the fix

title: "B" Camshaft Position Slow Response (Bank 2)
description: Variable valve timing (VarioCam) fault: during a commanded camshaft phase change, the actual position of the "B" camshaft on bank 2 (cylinders 4-6) responds too slowly to match the position the DME requested. Usually oil-flow or actuator related rather than a failed sensor.
causes: Low oil level, degraded oil, or wrong viscosity
Clogged camshaft oil control valve (VarioCam solenoid) or its filter screen
Stretched timing chain or worn chain tensioner/guides
Wiring or connector fault at the solenoid
diagnosis: Check oil level and condition; confirm correct grade (Porsche A40 / 0W-40)
Scan live data: compare commanded vs actual cam timing during a phase change
Test the camshaft oil control valve solenoid (resistance + actuation) and clean its screen
Inspect timing chain for stretch and the tensioner/guides for wear
Check solenoid wiring/connector continuity and the cam sensor signal
Clear the code and road-test to confirm the fix

title: "A" Camshaft Position Actuator Circuit (Bank 1)
description: The DME detected an electrical fault in the Bank 1 intake camshaft adjuster (VarioCam oil-control solenoid) circuit.
causes: Failed camshaft oil-control valve / VarioCam solenoid
Clogged oil galleries to the adjuster
diagnosis: Read live cam-adjustment data with a Porsche-capable tool
Measure solenoid resistance and circuit continuity

title: "A" Camshaft Position Timing Over-Advanced or System Performance (Bank 1)
description: Bank 1 camshaft timing is more advanced than commanded — a mechanical/hydraulic VarioCam performance fault rather than a pure circuit fault.
causes: Worst case: spun/worn cam timing on a Porsche flat-six
diagnosis: Clean or replace the oil control valve
Compare actual vs commanded cam angle in live data
If it persists, inspect timing components mechanically

title: "A" Camshaft Position Timing Over-Retarded (Bank 1)
description: Bank 1 camshaft timing is more retarded than commanded — a VarioCam performance fault.
diagnosis: Clean or replace the oil control valve
Compare actual vs commanded cam angle
Inspect timing mechanically if it persists

title: "B" Camshaft Position Actuator Circuit (Bank 1)
description: Electrical fault in the Bank 1 exhaust camshaft adjuster (oil-control solenoid) circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the camshaft adjuster solenoid connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: "B" Camshaft Position Timing Over-Advanced (Bank 1)
description: Bank 1 exhaust camshaft timing over-advanced — a VarioCam performance fault.
diagnosis: Compare actual vs commanded cam angle

title: Crankshaft Position - Camshaft Position Correlation (Bank 1 Sensor A)
description: The crankshaft and camshaft position signals are out of correlation on Bank 1 — possible cam-timing fault.
diagnosis: Compare cam vs crank correlation in live data
Verify cam timing mechanically if needed

title: Crankshaft Position - Camshaft Position Correlation (Bank 1 Sensor B)
description: Crank/cam correlation fault on Bank 1, exhaust-cam sensor.

title: Crankshaft Position - Camshaft Position Correlation (Bank 2 Sensor A)
description: Crank/cam correlation fault on Bank 2, intake-cam sensor.

title: Crankshaft Position - Camshaft Position Correlation (Bank 2 Sensor B)
description: Crank/cam correlation fault on Bank 2, exhaust-cam sensor.

title: "A" Camshaft Position Actuator Circuit (Bank 2)
description: Same as P0010 but for Bank 2 (cylinders 4-6) intake camshaft adjuster circuit.

title: "A" Camshaft Position Timing Over-Advanced or System Performance (Bank 2)
description: Bank 2 cam timing over-advanced / VarioCam performance fault. On Porsche flat-sixes a persistent P0021 can indicate a mechanical cam-timing problem, not merely a sensor.
causes: Failed adjuster or oil control valve
diagnosis: Replace oil control valve and cam sensor first
If it persists, inspect cam timing mechanically with specialist tooling

title: "A" Camshaft Position Timing Over-Retarded (Bank 2)
description: Bank 2 camshaft timing is more retarded than commanded — a VarioCam performance fault.
diagnosis: Clean or replace the oil control valve
Compare actual vs commanded cam angle
Inspect timing mechanically if it persists

title: "B" Camshaft Position Actuator Circuit (Bank 2)
description: Electrical fault in the Bank 2 exhaust camshaft adjuster circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the camshaft adjuster solenoid connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: "B" Camshaft Position Timing Over-Advanced (Bank 2)
description: Bank 2 exhaust camshaft timing over-advanced — a VarioCam performance fault.
diagnosis: Compare actual vs commanded cam angle

title: HO2S Heater Control Circuit (Bank 1 Sensor 1)
description: Fault in the heater control circuit for the upstream Bank 1 oxygen sensor.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the O2 sensor heater connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: HO2S Heater Control Circuit Low (Bank 1 Sensor 1)
description: Upstream Bank 1 O2 sensor heater circuit voltage low.
causes: Short to ground in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the O2 sensor heater wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for a short to ground
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: HO2S Heater Control Circuit High (Bank 1 Sensor 1)
description: Upstream Bank 1 O2 sensor heater circuit voltage high.
causes: Open circuit or short to power in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the O2 sensor heater wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for an open or short to power
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: HO2S Heater Control Circuit (Bank 1 Sensor 2)
description: Fault in the heater control circuit for the downstream Bank 1 oxygen sensor.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the O2 sensor heater connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: HO2S Heater Control Circuit Low (Bank 1 Sensor 2)
description: Downstream Bank 1 O2 sensor heater circuit voltage low.
causes: Short to ground in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the O2 sensor heater wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for a short to ground
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: HO2S Heater Control Circuit High (Bank 1 Sensor 2)
description: Downstream Bank 1 O2 sensor heater circuit voltage high.
causes: Open circuit or short to power in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the O2 sensor heater wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for an open or short to power
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: HO2S Heater Control Circuit (Bank 2 Sensor 1)
description: Fault in the heater control circuit for the upstream Bank 2 oxygen sensor.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the O2 sensor heater connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: HO2S Heater Control Circuit Low (Bank 2 Sensor 1)
description: Upstream Bank 2 O2 sensor heater circuit voltage low.
causes: Short to ground in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the O2 sensor heater wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for a short to ground
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: HO2S Heater Control Circuit High (Bank 2 Sensor 1)
description: Upstream Bank 2 O2 sensor heater circuit voltage high.
causes: Open circuit or short to power in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the O2 sensor heater wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for an open or short to power
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: HO2S Heater Control Circuit (Bank 2 Sensor 2)
description: Fault in the heater control circuit for the downstream Bank 2 oxygen sensor.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the O2 sensor heater connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Fuel Rail/System Pressure Too Low
description: Fuel rail/system pressure is below the expected level — on DFI engines this points to the high-pressure pump, regulator, or supply.
diagnosis: Check commanded vs actual rail pressure in live data
Test the high-pressure pump and regulator

title: Fuel Rail/System Pressure Too High
description: Fuel rail/system pressure is above the expected level on the DFI system.
diagnosis: Check commanded vs actual rail pressure

title: Fuel Pressure Regulator 1 Performance
description: The fuel pressure regulator/metering valve is not controlling rail pressure as expected.
diagnosis: Compare commanded vs actual rail pressure

title: Mass or Volume Air Flow Circuit Malfunction
description: General fault in the mass air flow (MAF) sensor circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the MAF sensor connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: MAF Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
description: The MAF signal is out of the expected range relative to other engine data.

title: MAF Sensor Circuit Low Input
description: The MAF signal voltage/frequency is too low.

title: Mass or Volume Air Flow Circuit High Input
description: The MAF signal voltage/frequency is too high.
causes: Open circuit or short to power in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the MAF sensor wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for an open or short to power
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: Mass or Volume Air Flow Circuit Intermittent
description: Intermittent fault in the MAF sensor circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the MAF sensor connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Manifold Absolute Pressure/Barometric Pressure Circuit Malfunction
description: General fault in the MAP/barometric pressure sensor circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the MAP sensor connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: MAP/Barometric Pressure Sensor Range/Performance
description: The manifold absolute pressure sensor reading is implausible. The 981 uses a MAP sensor in conjunction with the MAF.
diagnosis: Compare MAP vs barometric at key-on

title: Manifold Absolute Pressure/Barometric Pressure Circuit Low Input
description: MAP/baro sensor signal voltage is too low.
causes: Short to ground in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the MAP sensor wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for a short to ground
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: Manifold Absolute Pressure/Barometric Pressure Circuit High Input
description: MAP/baro sensor signal voltage is too high.
causes: Open circuit or short to power in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the MAP sensor wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for an open or short to power
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: Intake Air Temperature Sensor Circuit Malfunction
description: General fault in the intake air temperature (IAT) sensor circuit. IAT is typically integrated within the MAF on the 981.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the IAT sensor connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Intake Air Temperature Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
description: The IAT signal is implausible relative to other data.
diagnosis: Compare IAT sensor live data against other sensors for plausibility
Check for related mechanical causes (leaks, restriction)
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Intake Air Temperature Sensor Circuit Low Input
description: The IAT signal is stuck low. IAT is typically integrated within the MAF on the 981.

title: Intake Air Temperature Sensor Circuit High Input
description: The IAT signal is stuck high. IAT is typically integrated within the MAF on the 981.

title: Intake Air Temperature Sensor Circuit Intermittent
description: Intermittent fault in the IAT sensor circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the IAT sensor connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Engine Coolant Temperature Circuit Malfunction
description: General fault in the engine coolant temperature (ECT) sensor circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the ECT sensor connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Engine Coolant Temperature Sensor Range/Performance
description: The ECT signal is implausible relative to other data.
diagnosis: Compare ECT vs IAT at cold start

title: ECT Sensor Circuit Low Input
description: The coolant temperature signal is stuck low (reads too hot / short).

title: ECT Sensor Circuit High Input
description: The coolant temperature signal is stuck high (reads too cold / open).

title: Engine Coolant Temperature Circuit Intermittent
description: Intermittent fault in the ECT sensor circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the ECT sensor connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Throttle/Pedal Position Sensor/Switch "A" Circuit Malfunction
description: General fault in throttle/pedal position sensor "A". On the drive-by-wire 981 this can trigger reduced power.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the throttle position sensor connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Throttle/Pedal Position Sensor/Switch "A" Circuit Range/Performance
description: Throttle/pedal position sensor "A" reading is implausible.
causes: Faulty or drifting throttle position sensor
diagnosis: Compare throttle position sensor live data against other sensors for plausibility
Check for related mechanical causes (leaks, restriction)
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Throttle/Pedal Position Sensor "A" Circuit Low Input
description: The throttle position sensor signal is stuck low. On the drive-by-wire 981 this can trigger limp / reduced power.

title: Throttle/Pedal Position Sensor "A" Circuit High Input
description: The throttle position sensor signal is stuck high. On the drive-by-wire 981 this can trigger limp / reduced power.

title: Throttle/Pedal Position Sensor/Switch "A" Circuit Intermittent
description: Intermittent fault in throttle/pedal position sensor "A".
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the throttle position sensor connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Insufficient Coolant Temperature for Closed Loop Fuel Control
description: The engine has not reached a coolant temperature high enough to enter closed-loop fuel control in the expected time.
diagnosis: Monitor the warm-up curve in live data
Verify ECT sensor against IAT at cold start

title: Insufficient Coolant Temperature for Stable Operation
description: Coolant temperature is too low for stable engine operation.
diagnosis: Verify coolant level and warm-up curve

title: Coolant Thermostat (Coolant Temp Below Regulating Temperature)
description: The engine is not reaching normal operating temperature within the expected time — the thermostat is staying open.
symptoms: Low temp gauge on the highway

title: O2 Sensor Circuit Malfunction (Bank 1 Sensor 1)
description: Fault in the upstream (pre-cat) Bank 1 lambda sensor circuit or signal.

title: O2 Sensor Circuit Low Voltage (Bank 1 Sensor 1)
description: Upstream Bank 1 sensor voltage stuck low (lean / short to ground).
diagnosis: Verify fuel trims and exhaust integrity

title: O2 Sensor Circuit High Voltage (Bank 1 Sensor 1)
description: Upstream Bank 1 sensor voltage stuck high (rich / short to power).
diagnosis: Check wiring for short to power

title: O2 Sensor Circuit Slow Response (Bank 1 Sensor 1)
description: Upstream Bank 1 sensor switching too slowly — an aged or contaminated sensor.
diagnosis: Compare sensor response rate in live data

title: O2 Sensor Circuit No Activity Detected (Bank 1 Sensor 1)
description: No signal activity from the upstream Bank 1 O2 sensor (open circuit / dead sensor).

title: O2 Sensor Heater Circuit Malfunction (Bank 1 Sensor 1)
description: Fault in the heater element circuit of the upstream Bank 1 O2 sensor.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the O2 sensor heater connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: O2 Sensor Circuit Malfunction (Bank 1 Sensor 2)
description: Fault in the downstream (post-cat) Bank 1 O2 sensor circuit. Usually no drivability change.
diagnosis: View live data on the rear sensor

title: O2 Sensor Circuit Low Voltage (Bank 1 Sensor 2)
description: Downstream Bank 1 sensor stuck low.

title: O2 Sensor Circuit High Voltage (Bank 1 Sensor 2)
description: Downstream Bank 1 sensor stuck high.

title: O2 Sensor Circuit Slow Response (Bank 1 Sensor 2)
description: Downstream Bank 1 O2 sensor switching too slowly.
diagnosis: Compare sensor response in live data

title: O2 Sensor Circuit No Activity Detected (Bank 1 Sensor 2)
description: No signal activity from the downstream Bank 1 sensor (open circuit / dead sensor).

title: O2 Sensor Heater Circuit Malfunction (Bank 1 Sensor 2)
description: Fault in the heater element circuit of the downstream Bank 1 O2 sensor.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the O2 sensor heater connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: O2 Sensor Circuit Malfunction (Bank 2 Sensor 1)
description: Fault in the upstream (pre-cat) Bank 2 lambda sensor circuit or signal.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the O2 sensor connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: O2 Sensor Circuit Low Voltage (Bank 2 Sensor 1)
description: Upstream Bank 2 sensor voltage stuck low — mirror of P0131 on the opposite bank.
diagnosis: Verify fuel trims and exhaust integrity

title: O2 Sensor Circuit High Voltage (Bank 2 Sensor 1)
description: Upstream Bank 2 sensor voltage stuck high (rich / short to power).
causes: Open circuit or short to power in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the O2 sensor wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for an open or short to power
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: O2 Sensor Circuit Slow Response (Bank 2 Sensor 1)
description: Upstream Bank 2 sensor switching too slowly — mirror of P0133.
diagnosis: Compare sensor response rate in live data

title: O2 Sensor Circuit No Activity Detected (Bank 2 Sensor 1)
description: No signal activity from the upstream Bank 2 O2 sensor.

title: O2 Sensor Heater Circuit Malfunction (Bank 2 Sensor 1)
description: Fault in the heater element circuit of the upstream Bank 2 O2 sensor.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the O2 sensor heater connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: O2 Sensor Circuit Malfunction (Bank 2 Sensor 2)
description: Fault in the downstream (post-cat) Bank 2 O2 sensor circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the O2 sensor connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: O2 Sensor Circuit Low Voltage (Bank 2 Sensor 2)
description: Downstream Bank 2 sensor voltage stuck low.
causes: Short to ground in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the O2 sensor wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for a short to ground
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: O2 Sensor Circuit High Voltage (Bank 2 Sensor 2)
description: Downstream Bank 2 sensor voltage stuck high.
causes: Open circuit or short to power in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the O2 sensor wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for an open or short to power
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: O2 Sensor Circuit No Activity Detected (Bank 2 Sensor 2)
description: No signal activity from the downstream Bank 2 O2 sensor.

title: O2 Sensor Heater Circuit Malfunction (Bank 2 Sensor 2)
description: Fault in the heater element circuit of the downstream Bank 2 O2 sensor.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the O2 sensor heater connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Fuel Trim Malfunction (Bank 1)
description: Bank 1 fuel trim is out of the normal correction range (too lean or too rich).
diagnosis: Read short- and long-term fuel trims
Check MAF and O2 live data

title: System Too Lean (Bank 1)
description: Long-term fuel trim on Bank 1 exceeded the lean threshold; the DME is adding maximum fuel to compensate.

title: System Too Rich (Bank 1)
description: Long-term fuel trim on Bank 1 indicates an over-rich mixture; the DME is removing fuel to compensate.

title: Fuel Trim Malfunction (Bank 2)
description: Bank 2 fuel trim is out of the normal correction range.
diagnosis: Read short- and long-term fuel trims
Check MAF and O2 live data

title: System Too Lean (Bank 2)
description: Same as P0171 for Bank 2. Often appears together with P0171, pointing to a shared/global cause such as the MAF, fuel pressure, or a large vacuum leak.
diagnosis: Read fuel trims on both banks

title: System Too Rich (Bank 2)
description: Same as P0172 for Bank 2 — an over-rich mixture fuel trim cannot fully correct.
diagnosis: Read fuel trims on both banks

title: Fuel Rail Pressure Sensor Circuit Malfunction
description: Fault in the (high-pressure) fuel rail pressure sensor circuit — relevant to the DFI direct-injection 9A1/MA1 engines.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the fuel rail pressure sensor connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Fuel Rail Pressure Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
description: The high-pressure fuel rail pressure sensor reading is implausible.
causes: Faulty or drifting fuel rail pressure sensor
diagnosis: Compare fuel rail pressure sensor live data against other sensors for plausibility
Check for related mechanical causes (leaks, restriction)
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Injector Circuit/Open - Cylinder 1
description: Fault in the fuel injector control circuit for cylinder 1.
causes: Driver circuit fault in the ECM
diagnosis: Measure fuel injector resistance and check wiring
Swap to confirm and replace if faulty

title: Injector Circuit/Open - Cylinder 2
description: Fault in the fuel injector control circuit for cylinder 2.
causes: Driver circuit fault in the ECM
diagnosis: Measure fuel injector resistance and check wiring
Swap to confirm and replace if faulty

title: Injector Circuit/Open - Cylinder 3
description: Fault in the fuel injector control circuit for cylinder 3.
causes: Driver circuit fault in the ECM
diagnosis: Measure fuel injector resistance and check wiring
Swap to confirm and replace if faulty

title: Injector Circuit/Open - Cylinder 4
description: Fault in the fuel injector control circuit for cylinder 4.
causes: Driver circuit fault in the ECM
diagnosis: Measure fuel injector resistance and check wiring
Swap to confirm and replace if faulty

title: Injector Circuit/Open - Cylinder 5
description: Fault in the fuel injector control circuit for cylinder 5.
causes: Driver circuit fault in the ECM
diagnosis: Measure fuel injector resistance and check wiring
Swap to confirm and replace if faulty

title: Injector Circuit/Open - Cylinder 6
description: Fault in the fuel injector control circuit for cylinder 6.
causes: Driver circuit fault in the ECM
diagnosis: Measure fuel injector resistance and check wiring
Swap to confirm and replace if faulty

title: Engine Coolant Over Temperature Condition
description: The engine has exceeded its safe coolant temperature — risk of serious engine damage.
diagnosis: Test water pump, thermostat, and fans

title: Turbocharger/Supercharger Overboost Condition
description: Boost pressure exceeds the expected level. Applies only to forced-induction variants.

title: Turbocharger/Supercharger Underboost Condition
description: Boost pressure is below the expected level. Applies only to forced-induction variants.

title: Random/Multiple Cylinder Misfire Detected
description: The DME detected misfires across multiple or random cylinders. Unburned fuel reaching the catalysts risks expensive converter damage.
diagnosis: Check fuel pressure and fuel trims

title: Cylinder 1 Misfire Detected
description: Misfire isolated to cylinder 1. On the 981 flat-six, cylinders 1-3 form Bank 1.
causes: Faulty coil or plug on cylinder 1
diagnosis: Swap the coil and plug to an adjacent cylinder and see if the misfire follows

title: Cylinder 2 Misfire Detected
description: Misfire isolated to cylinder 2 (Bank 1).
causes: Faulty coil or plug on cylinder 2
diagnosis: Swap coil and plug to an adjacent cylinder; see if misfire follows

title: Cylinder 3 Misfire Detected
description: Misfire isolated to cylinder 3 (Bank 1).
causes: Faulty coil or plug on cylinder 3
diagnosis: Swap coil and plug to an adjacent cylinder; see if misfire follows

title: Cylinder 4 Misfire Detected
description: Misfire isolated to cylinder 4. On the 981 flat-six, cylinders 4-6 form Bank 2.
causes: Faulty coil or plug on cylinder 4
diagnosis: Swap coil and plug to an adjacent cylinder; see if misfire follows

title: Cylinder 5 Misfire Detected
description: Misfire isolated to cylinder 5 (Bank 2).
causes: Faulty coil or plug on cylinder 5
diagnosis: Swap coil and plug to an adjacent cylinder; see if misfire follows

title: Cylinder 6 Misfire Detected
description: Misfire isolated to cylinder 6 (Bank 2).
causes: Faulty coil or plug on cylinder 6
diagnosis: Swap coil and plug to an adjacent cylinder; see if misfire follows

title: Misfire Detected on Startup (First 1000 Revolutions)
description: Misfire detected shortly after engine start.

title: Knock Sensor 1 Circuit Range/Performance (Bank 1)
description: Knock sensor 1 signal is implausible. The DME may retard timing as a safeguard.
diagnosis: Compare knock sensor live data against other sensors for plausibility
Check for related mechanical causes (leaks, restriction)
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Knock Sensor 1 Circuit Low Input (Bank 1)
description: Knock sensor 1 signal voltage is too low (short to ground / dead sensor). The DME loses knock data and retards timing as a safeguard.
diagnosis: Verify torque on the sensor mount

title: Knock Sensor 1 Circuit High Input (Bank 1)
description: Knock sensor 1 signal voltage is too high (open circuit / short to power).
diagnosis: Check wiring for open/short to power

title: Knock Sensor 2 Circuit Malfunction (Bank 2)
description: Fault in the knock sensor 2 (Bank 2) signal circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the knock sensor connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Knock Sensor 2 Circuit Low Input (Bank 2)
description: Knock sensor 2 signal voltage too low.
causes: Short to ground in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the knock sensor wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for a short to ground
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: Knock Sensor 2 Circuit High Input (Bank 2)
description: Knock sensor 2 signal voltage too high.
causes: Open circuit or short to power in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the knock sensor wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for an open or short to power
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: Crankshaft Position Sensor "A" Circuit Malfunction
description: Fault in the crankshaft position sensor signal — critical for ignition timing and injection.
diagnosis: Scope the sensor signal while cranking
Inspect tone ring and air gap

title: Crankshaft Position Sensor "A" Circuit Range/Performance
description: Crankshaft position sensor signal is implausible — affects ignition timing and injection.
diagnosis: Inspect tone ring and air gap

title: Camshaft Position Sensor "A" Circuit Malfunction (Bank 1)
description: Fault in the camshaft position sensor signal — affects sync, starting, and VarioCam control.
diagnosis: Scope cam vs crank signal correlation

title: Camshaft Position Sensor "A" Circuit Range/Performance (Bank 1)
description: Camshaft position sensor signal is out of range or loses sync with the crank signal.

title: Camshaft Position Sensor "A" Circuit (Bank 2)
description: Fault in the Bank 2 camshaft position sensor signal.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the camshaft position sensor connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Ignition Coil "A" Primary/Secondary Circuit Malfunction
description: Fault in the cylinder 1 ignition coil primary/secondary circuit.
causes: Driver circuit fault in the ECM

title: Ignition Coil "B" Primary/Secondary Circuit Malfunction
description: Fault in the cylinder 2 ignition coil primary/secondary circuit.
causes: Driver circuit fault in the ECM

title: Ignition Coil "C" Primary/Secondary Circuit Malfunction
description: Fault in the cylinder 3 ignition coil primary/secondary circuit.
causes: Driver circuit fault in the ECM

title: Ignition Coil "D" Primary/Secondary Circuit Malfunction
description: Fault in the cylinder 4 ignition coil primary/secondary circuit.
causes: Driver circuit fault in the ECM

title: Ignition Coil "E" Primary/Secondary Circuit Malfunction
description: Fault in the cylinder 5 ignition coil primary/secondary circuit.
causes: Driver circuit fault in the ECM

title: Ignition Coil "F" Primary/Secondary Circuit Malfunction
description: Fault in the cylinder 6 ignition coil primary/secondary circuit.
causes: Driver circuit fault in the ECM

title: Exhaust Gas Recirculation Flow Insufficient Detected
description: Insufficient EGR flow detected. Note: many naturally-aspirated 981 flat-sixes do not use EGR; applicability is engine-specific.
diagnosis: Test EGR operation in live data

title: Secondary Air Injection System Malfunction
description: General fault in the secondary air injection system used to reduce cold-start emissions.

title: Secondary Air Injection System Incorrect Flow Detected
description: The secondary air injection system flow is not within the expected range during cold start.
diagnosis: Inspect the combination valve and hoses

title: Secondary Air Injection System Switching Valve "A" Circuit Open
description: Open circuit on the secondary air injection switching valve.
causes: Open circuit or short to power in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the secondary air switching valve wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for an open or short to power
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: Secondary Air Injection System Relay "A" Circuit Malfunction
description: Fault in the secondary air injection pump relay control circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the secondary air relay connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Catalyst System Efficiency Below Threshold (Bank 1)
description: The Bank 1 catalytic converter is no longer converting efficiently (the rear O2 trace mirrors the front).
causes: Upstream misfire/oil contamination that killed the cat
diagnosis: Confirm there are no misfire or lean codes first
Compare front vs rear O2 traces

title: Warm Up Catalyst Efficiency Below Threshold (Bank 1)
description: The Bank 1 warm-up catalyst is below its efficiency threshold.
diagnosis: Compare front vs rear O2 traces

title: Main Catalyst Efficiency Below Threshold (Bank 1)
description: The Bank 1 main catalyst is below its efficiency threshold.
diagnosis: Compare front vs rear O2 traces

title: Catalyst System Efficiency Below Threshold (Bank 2)
description: Same as P0420 for Bank 2 — the catalyst is no longer converting efficiently.
diagnosis: Confirm no misfire or lean codes first
Compare front vs rear O2 traces

title: Warm Up Catalyst Efficiency Below Threshold (Bank 2)
description: The Bank 2 warm-up catalyst is below its efficiency threshold.
diagnosis: Compare front vs rear O2 traces

title: EVAP System Malfunction (general)
description: General fault in the evaporative emissions system (a leak or component fault).

title: EVAP System Incorrect Purge Flow
description: EVAP purge flow is not within the expected range.

title: EVAP System Leak Detected (Small Leak)
description: A small leak in the EVAP system.
diagnosis: Inspect and reseat the gas cap

title: EVAP System Purge Control Valve Circuit Malfunction
description: Electrical fault in the EVAP purge control valve circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the EVAP purge valve connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: EVAP Purge Control Valve Circuit Open
description: Open circuit on the EVAP purge control valve.
causes: Open circuit or short to power in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the EVAP purge valve wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for an open or short to power
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: EVAP Purge Control Valve Circuit Shorted
description: Short circuit on the EVAP purge control valve.
causes: Short to ground in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the EVAP purge valve wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for a short to ground
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: EVAP System Vent Control Circuit Malfunction
description: Fault in the EVAP vent control valve circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the EVAP vent valve connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: EVAP System Vent Control Circuit Open
description: Open circuit on the EVAP vent control valve.
causes: Open circuit or short to power in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the EVAP vent valve wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for an open or short to power
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: EVAP System Vent Valve/Solenoid Circuit Malfunction
description: Fault in the EVAP vent valve/solenoid circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the EVAP vent solenoid connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: EVAP System Pressure Sensor Malfunction
description: Fault in the EVAP system pressure sensor.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the EVAP pressure sensor connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: EVAP System Pressure Sensor Range/Performance
description: EVAP pressure sensor reading is implausible.
causes: Faulty or drifting EVAP pressure sensor
diagnosis: Compare EVAP pressure sensor live data against other sensors for plausibility
Check for related mechanical causes (leaks, restriction)
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: EVAP System Leak Detected (Large/Gross Leak)
description: A large leak detected in the EVAP system.
diagnosis: Check gas cap presence and seal

title: EVAP System Leak Detected (Very Small Leak)
description: A very small (tight) leak in the EVAP system.
diagnosis: Tighten or replace the gas cap

title: EVAP System Leak Detected (Fuel Cap Loose/Off)
description: EVAP leak attributed to a loose or missing fuel filler cap.
diagnosis: Test purge and vent valve operation
Inspect the charcoal canister and hoses

title: EVAP System Purge Control Valve Circuit Low
description: EVAP purge control valve circuit voltage low.
causes: Short to ground in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the EVAP purge valve wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for a short to ground
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: EVAP System Purge Control Valve Circuit High
description: EVAP purge control valve circuit voltage high.
causes: Open circuit or short to power in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the EVAP purge valve wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for an open or short to power
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: Fuel Level Sensor Circuit Malfunction
description: Fault in the fuel level sensor circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the fuel level sensor connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Fuel Level Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
description: Fuel level sensor reading is implausible (e.g., stuck).
causes: Faulty or drifting fuel level sensor
diagnosis: Compare fuel level sensor live data against other sensors for plausibility
Check for related mechanical causes (leaks, restriction)
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Fuel Level Sensor Circuit Low Input
description: Fuel level sensor signal too low.
causes: Short to ground in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the fuel level sensor wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for a short to ground
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: Fuel Level Sensor Circuit High Input
description: Fuel level sensor signal too high.
causes: Open circuit or short to power in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the fuel level sensor wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for an open or short to power
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: Cooling Fan 1 Control Circuit Malfunction
description: Fault in the cooling fan 1 control circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the cooling fan relay/control connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Cooling Fan 2 Control Circuit Malfunction
description: Fault in the cooling fan 2 control circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the cooling fan relay/control connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Vehicle Speed Sensor Malfunction
description: Fault in the vehicle speed signal.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the vehicle speed sensor connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Vehicle Speed Sensor Range/Performance
description: Vehicle speed signal is implausible.
causes: Faulty or drifting vehicle speed sensor
diagnosis: Compare vehicle speed sensor live data against other sensors for plausibility
Check for related mechanical causes (leaks, restriction)
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Idle Air Control System Malfunction
description: Fault in idle speed control. On the drive-by-wire 981 idle is managed by the electronic throttle.
diagnosis: Check throttle adaptation in live data

title: Idle Air Control System RPM Lower Than Expected
description: Measured idle RPM is lower than the commanded idle.
diagnosis: Check idle live data and adaptation

title: Idle Air Control System RPM Higher Than Expected
description: Measured idle RPM is higher than the commanded idle.
diagnosis: Check idle adaptation in live data

title: Engine Oil Pressure Sensor/Switch Range/Performance
description: The engine oil pressure sensor reading is implausible. Low oil pressure can cause severe engine damage.
diagnosis: Verify with a mechanical oil-pressure gauge before condemning the engine

title: Engine Oil Pressure Sensor/Switch Low Voltage
description: Oil pressure sensor signal low — may indicate low oil pressure.

title: Engine Oil Pressure Sensor/Switch High Voltage
description: Oil pressure sensor signal high.
causes: Open circuit or short to power in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the oil pressure sensor wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for an open or short to power
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: System Voltage Malfunction
description: The control module detected an out-of-range system (battery) voltage.

title: System Voltage Low
description: System voltage is below the expected range.

title: System Voltage High
description: System voltage is above the expected range.

title: Brake Switch "A" Circuit Malfunction
description: Fault in the brake light/brake switch signal used by the powertrain control.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the brake light switch connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Serial Communication Link Malfunction
description: Fault in a serial communication (data bus) link between control modules.
diagnosis: Scan all modules for communication faults

title: Internal Control Module Memory Check Sum Error
description: The control module detected an internal memory checksum error — an internal ECM/PCM fault.
diagnosis: Replace and code the module if confirmed

title: Control Module Programming Error
description: The control module was not programmed correctly.

title: Internal Control Module RAM Error
description: The control module detected an internal random-access memory (RAM) error.
diagnosis: Replace and code the module if it persists

title: Internal Control Module ROM Error
description: The control module detected an internal read-only memory (ROM) error.
diagnosis: Replace and code the module if it persists

title: ECM/PCM Processor Fault
description: Internal processor fault within the engine/powertrain control module.
diagnosis: Replace and code the module if confirmed

title: Control Module Performance
description: Internal performance fault within the control module.

title: Control Module Vehicle Options Error
description: The control module detected an error in stored vehicle option/configuration data.

title: Starter Relay Circuit Malfunction
description: Fault in the starter relay control circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the starter relay connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Generator Control Circuit Malfunction
description: Fault in the generator (alternator) control circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the alternator control connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Generator Field Terminal Circuit Low
description: Alternator field terminal control circuit voltage low.
causes: Short to ground in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the alternator field circuit wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for a short to ground
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: Generator Field Terminal Circuit High
description: Alternator field terminal control circuit voltage high.
causes: Open circuit or short to power in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the alternator field circuit wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for an open or short to power
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: Fuel Pump "A" Control Circuit Open
description: Open circuit in the fuel pump control.
diagnosis: Check fuel pump control circuit and relay

title: Fuel Pump "A" Control Circuit Low
description: Fuel pump control circuit voltage low.
causes: Short to ground in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the fuel pump control wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for a short to ground
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: Fuel Pump "A" Control Circuit High
description: Fuel pump control circuit voltage high.
causes: Open circuit or short to power in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the fuel pump control wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for an open or short to power
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: Sensor Reference Voltage "A" Circuit Open
description: Open circuit on a sensor 5V reference supply from the control module.
causes: Short or open on the reference circuit
Shorted sensor pulling the reference down
diagnosis: Check the reference circuit for shorts/opens

title: A/C Clutch Relay Control Circuit Malfunction
description: Fault in the A/C compressor clutch relay control circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the A/C clutch relay connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Malfunction Indicator Lamp (MIL) Control Circuit Malfunction
description: Fault in the check-engine (MIL) lamp control circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the MIL control circuit connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: ECM/PCM Power Relay Control Circuit Open
description: Open circuit in the main control-module power relay control.
diagnosis: Inspect power and grounds to the module

title: Transmission Control System Malfunction (MIL Request)
description: A generic flag that the PDK transmission control unit has stored one or more of its own fault codes and requested the check-engine light. P0700 itself carries no detail — a Porsche-capable tool (PIWIS/Durametric) reads the manufacturer sub-code that identifies the actual fault.
diagnosis: Read TCU-specific sub-codes with a Porsche-capable scanner
Check PDK fluid level/condition and service history

title: Transmission Control System Range/Performance
description: The transmission control system is performing outside its expected range. Read the TCU sub-code with a Porsche-capable tool.
diagnosis: Read TCU-specific sub-codes with a Porsche-capable scanner
Check fluid level/condition and service history

title: Transmission Control System Electrical
description: Electrical fault in the transmission control system.
diagnosis: Read TCU sub-codes with a Porsche-capable tool

title: Transmission Range Sensor Circuit Malfunction (PRNDL Input)
description: Fault in the transmission range (gear selector position) sensor circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the transmission range sensor connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Transmission Range Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
description: The transmission range sensor reading is implausible.
causes: Faulty or drifting transmission range sensor
diagnosis: Compare transmission range sensor live data against other sensors for plausibility
Check for related mechanical causes (leaks, restriction)
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor Range/Performance
description: The transmission fluid temperature sensor reading is implausible.
causes: Faulty or drifting transmission fluid temperature sensor
diagnosis: Compare transmission fluid temperature sensor live data against other sensors for plausibility
Check for related mechanical causes (leaks, restriction)
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor Circuit Low Input
description: Transmission fluid temperature signal stuck low.
causes: Short to ground in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the transmission fluid temperature sensor wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for a short to ground
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: Transmission Fluid Temperature Sensor Circuit High Input
description: Transmission fluid temperature signal stuck high.
causes: Open circuit or short to power in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the transmission fluid temperature sensor wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for an open or short to power
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: Input/Turbine Speed Sensor Circuit Malfunction
description: Fault in the transmission input/turbine speed sensor circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the input/turbine speed sensor connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Input/Turbine Speed Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
description: Input/turbine speed sensor reading is implausible.
causes: Faulty or drifting input/turbine speed sensor
diagnosis: Compare input/turbine speed sensor live data against other sensors for plausibility
Check for related mechanical causes (leaks, restriction)
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Input/Turbine Speed Sensor Circuit No Signal
description: No signal from the input/turbine speed sensor.

title: Output Speed Sensor Circuit Malfunction
description: Fault in the transmission output speed sensor circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the output speed sensor connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Output Speed Sensor Circuit Range/Performance
description: Output speed sensor reading is implausible.
causes: Faulty or drifting output speed sensor
diagnosis: Compare output speed sensor live data against other sensors for plausibility
Check for related mechanical causes (leaks, restriction)
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Output Speed Sensor Circuit No Signal
description: No signal from the transmission output speed sensor.

title: Incorrect Gear Ratio
description: A measured gear ratio does not match the commanded gear.
diagnosis: Read TCU sub-codes with a Porsche-capable tool
Compare commanded vs actual gear in live data

title: Torque Converter Clutch Circuit Performance or Stuck Off
description: The torque-converter/clutch lock-up is not engaging as commanded. On PDK this maps to clutch engagement control.

title: Pressure Control Solenoid "A" Performance or Stuck Off
description: A transmission pressure control solenoid is stuck or underperforming.

title: Pressure Control Solenoid "A" Electrical
description: Electrical fault in a transmission pressure control solenoid circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the pressure control solenoid connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Shift Solenoid "A" Electrical
description: Electrical fault in shift solenoid "A".
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the shift solenoid connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Shift Solenoid "B" Electrical
description: Electrical fault in shift solenoid "B".
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the shift solenoid connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Pressure Control Solenoid "B" Performance or Stuck Off
description: Transmission pressure control solenoid "B" stuck or underperforming.

title: Transmission Fluid Pressure Sensor/Switch "A" Circuit Malfunction
description: Fault in a transmission fluid pressure sensor/switch circuit.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the transmission fluid pressure sensor connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Park/Neutral Switch Input Circuit Malfunction
description: Fault in the park/neutral position switch input.
causes: Loss of reference voltage or ground
diagnosis: Inspect the park/neutral switch connector and wiring for damage or corrosion
Check reference voltage and ground at the connector
Compare live data to a known-good value
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: Transmission Fluid Pressure Low
description: Transmission line pressure is below the expected range.
diagnosis: Measure line pressure in live data

title: Oxygen Sensing Adaptation, Bank 1 - Above Limit (Porsche-specific)
description: Porsche-specific: Bank 1 (cylinders 1-3) adaptive fuel trim has reached its maximum limit adding fuel — the mixture is so lean the DME cannot compensate. Note: many forums wrongly call P1128/P1130 separate banks; per PCA both are Bank 1 (P1128 = above limit / too lean).
causes: Damaged or dirty MAF (often after disturbing the air-filter box or using an over-oiled aftermarket filter)
diagnosis: Check fuel pressure and purge valve

title: Oxygen Sensing Adaptation, Bank 1 - Below Limit (Porsche-specific)
description: Porsche-specific: Bank 1 adaptive fuel trim reached its maximum limit leaning out — running too rich. Commonly pairs with P1128 when the MAF is faulty. Both P1128 and P1130 are Bank 1, not separate banks.

title: "A" Camshaft Position Actuator Control Circuit Low (Bank 1)
description: Camshaft adjuster (oil-control valve) control circuit voltage low, Bank 1.
causes: Short to ground in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the camshaft adjuster solenoid wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for a short to ground
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: "A" Camshaft Position Actuator Control Circuit High (Bank 1)
description: Camshaft adjuster control circuit voltage high, Bank 1.
causes: Open circuit or short to power in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the camshaft adjuster solenoid wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for an open or short to power
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: "B" Camshaft Position Actuator Control Circuit Low (Bank 1)
description: Exhaust camshaft adjuster control circuit voltage low, Bank 1.
causes: Short to ground in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the camshaft adjuster solenoid wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for a short to ground
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: "B" Camshaft Position Actuator Control Circuit High (Bank 1)
description: Exhaust camshaft adjuster control circuit voltage high, Bank 1.
causes: Open circuit or short to power in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the camshaft adjuster solenoid wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for an open or short to power
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: Post-Catalyst Fuel Trim System Too Lean (Bank 1)
description: The post-catalyst (rear) O2 sensor on Bank 1 reports a persistently lean condition that fuel trim cannot correct.
causes: Exhaust leak ahead of the rear sensor

title: Post-Catalyst Fuel Trim System Too Rich (Bank 1)
description: The post-catalyst (rear) O2 sensor on Bank 1 reports a persistently rich condition.

title: Post-Catalyst Fuel Trim System Too Lean (Bank 2)
description: Same as P2096 for Bank 2 — post-cat fuel trim cannot correct a lean condition.
causes: Exhaust leak ahead of the rear sensor

title: Post-Catalyst Fuel Trim System Too Rich (Bank 2)
description: Same as P2097 for Bank 2 — post-cat fuel trim indicates a persistently rich condition.

title: Throttle Actuator Control Motor Current Range/Performance
description: The throttle actuator (drive-by-wire) motor current is out of range.

title: Throttle Actuator Control Throttle Body Range/Performance
description: The electronic throttle body is not responding within the expected range.
diagnosis: Replace the throttle body if faulty

title: Throttle/Pedal Position Sensor/Switch "D" Circuit Low Input
description: Accelerator pedal position sensor "D" circuit voltage low.
causes: Short to ground in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the accelerator pedal position sensor wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for a short to ground
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: Throttle/Pedal Position Sensor/Switch "D" Circuit High Input
description: Accelerator pedal position sensor "D" circuit voltage high.
causes: Open circuit or short to power in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the accelerator pedal position sensor wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for an open or short to power
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: Throttle/Pedal Position Sensor/Switch "E" Circuit Low Input
description: Accelerator pedal position sensor "E" circuit voltage low.
causes: Short to ground in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the accelerator pedal position sensor wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for a short to ground
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: Throttle/Pedal Position Sensor/Switch "E" Circuit High Input
description: Accelerator pedal position sensor "E" circuit voltage high.
causes: Open circuit or short to power in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the accelerator pedal position sensor wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for an open or short to power
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: Throttle/Pedal Position Sensor "A"/"B" Voltage Correlation
description: The two throttle position sensor signals disagree.
diagnosis: Compare both TPS signals in live data

title: Throttle/Pedal Position Sensor "D"/"E" Voltage Correlation
description: The two accelerator pedal position sensor signals disagree.
diagnosis: Compare both pedal signals in live data

title: System Too Lean Off Idle (Bank 1)
description: Bank 1 fuel mixture is too lean during off-idle/part-throttle operation.

title: System Too Rich Off Idle (Bank 1)
description: Bank 1 fuel mixture is too rich during off-idle/part-throttle operation.

title: System Too Lean at Idle (Bank 1)
description: Bank 1 fuel mixture is too lean at idle.

title: System Too Rich at Idle (Bank 1)
description: Bank 1 fuel mixture is too rich at idle.

title: System Too Lean at Higher Load (Bank 1)
description: Bank 1 fuel mixture is too lean under higher load.

title: O2 Sensor Signal Stuck Lean (Bank 1 Sensor 1)
description: The upstream Bank 1 O2 sensor signal is biased/stuck lean.

title: O2 Sensor Signal Stuck Rich (Bank 1 Sensor 1)
description: The upstream Bank 1 O2 sensor signal is biased/stuck rich.

title: NOx Sensor Circuit Range/Performance (Bank 1)
description: NOx sensor reading is implausible. Applicability is engine/market-specific.
diagnosis: Compare NOx sensor live data against other sensors for plausibility
Check for related mechanical causes (leaks, restriction)
Replace the sensor if out of spec

title: O2 Sensor Signal Stuck Lean (Bank 1 Sensor 2)
description: The downstream Bank 1 sensor signal is stuck lean — a sensor or exhaust condition. Often no drivability change.
causes: Rarely a cat or fuel issue

title: O2 Sensor Signal Stuck Rich (Bank 1 Sensor 2)
description: The downstream Bank 1 sensor signal is stuck rich — a sensor or exhaust condition.
causes: Rarely a cat or fuel issue

title: EVAP Leak Detection Pump Control Circuit Open
description: Open circuit in the EVAP leak-detection pump control.
causes: Open circuit or short to power in the signal wire
diagnosis: Inspect the EVAP leak-detection pump wiring and connector
Check the signal circuit for an open or short to power
Replace the component if confirmed faulty

title: O2 Sensor Exhaust Sample Error (Bank 1 Sensor 1)
description: The upstream Bank 1 O2 sensor exhaust sample is faulty (e.g., a leak at the sensor).
diagnosis: Inspect for exhaust leaks at the sensor

title: EVAP Vent Valve Stuck Closed
description: The EVAP vent valve is stuck closed.
